items.title: Improving statistical reporting in psychology
items.author: Daniel S. Quintana
items.content: Practical guidelines for transparent statistical reporting in quantitative psychology are presented, covering key decisions from study planning through results reporting across frequentist, Bayesian, and sequential frameworks. Resources include the Transparent Statistical Reporting in Psychology (TSRP) Checklist as well as specific R packages, functions, and side-by-side comparisons of insufficient versus best-practice reporting.

items.title: Brief empathy interventions online can decrease but not increase empathic tendencies
items.author: Philip Pärnamets
items.content: Editorial Summary: Across five online studies, motivated empathy interventions for intergroup contexts failed to increase empathy or prosociality with outgroup members; participants were more easily motivated to decrease empathy than increase it.

items.title: Asymmetric learning and adaptability to changes in relational structure during transitive inference
items.author: Bernhard Spitzer
items.content: Human relational learning is winner-biased: we update our beliefs about winners more than losers. A transitive inference study with computational modelling shows this bias hinders flexibility when adapting to certain relational changes over others.

items.title: Psychological interventions that decrease psychological distance or challenge system justification increase motivation to exert effort to mitigate climate change
items.author: Patricia L. Lockwood
items.content: Pro-environmental actions often require effort. Participants were less motivated to help the climate than a food charity, but two interventions removed this bias. Computational modelling linked climate motivation with pro-environmental traits

items.title: The non-opponent nature of colour afterimages
items.author: Christoph Witzel
items.content: Three independent experiments establish a direct link between the perceived colours of afterimages and their physiological origin. Afterimages are not colour-opponent but cluster at cone photoreceptor sensitivity peaks.

items.title: Trait emotion regulation predicts momentary self-esteem level and variability in adolescents’ daily lives
items.author: Jenny Wagner
items.content: Analyzing experience sampling data from 408 adolescents, this study shows that the emotion regulation strategies reappraisal, reflection, social sharing, and expressive suppression are differentially related to momentary self-esteem level and variability.

items.title: Neural evidence for the effects of behavioral memory updating following fear conditioning
items.author: Laura E. Simons
items.content: This study examined whether behavioral memory updating can reduce fear in adolescents with and without chronic pain. While self-reported fear did not differ, neural data revealed distinct vmPFC-amygdala connectivity supporting memory updating.

items.title: Personal agency and social connection are associated with loneliness over time
items.author: Todd Chan
items.content: Longitudinal study shows loneliness is associated with distinct patterns of social connection and personal agency. Four prototypes identified across waves predict loneliness levels, with transitions among them associated with loneliness changes.
